title: Result
description: A typed output from a Task. The consumer (possibly another agent) MUST be able to act on this without re-parsing prose. Free-text summaries live inside 'output' under a schema-defined field, not as the Result itself.

result_id.description: Content-addressed id: hash(task_id, output, provenance.step_id). Two identical productions of the same Task yield identical result_ids.

status.description: 'partial' means the output is usable but incomplete (e.g., budget hit before all items processed).

output.description: Kind-specific output payload. MUST validate against the kind's output schema at the Task's declared version. Absent iff status == 'error'.

error.description: Present iff status != 'ok'. Present alongside 'output' when status == 'partial'.

code.description: Stable machine-readable error code (e.g., 'budget.exceeded', 'input.invalid', 'capability.denied'). Agents branch on this; humans read 'message'.

retryable.description: Whether retrying with the same inputs could succeed (transient vs. permanent failure).

confidence.description: Producer's self-reported confidence in 'output'. Consumers may threshold on this before acting.

evidence.description: Claims in 'output' supported by citations. Enables verification and training.

provenance.description: Who produced this Result. Different from the Task's provenance (which identifies the caller).
